pages.full_text:
leave them in residential summer camp for 3 weeks. They stopped fighting from 11.45 until 8. pm.
	After lunch we walked along the road to the Caves of the __Font de Gaume__, but there was a 3 day wait to get in. They only allow 375 people per day inside, & they've the best caves after Lascaux, which are now closed. We went on a 6 mile drive to a very crowded lake. Simon didn't like the look of what was floating in the water. Charles waded knee-deep out to a slide & shot down it into deep water! He did look indignant when his ring brought him up to the surface. John swims with one arm-band, so that I can see him.
	We returned to Les Eyzies to see the caving museum- Scrapers, statues
pages.notes: Text continues from previous spread; no handwritten page number visible at top-left. Text continues onto right page. 'Font de Gaume' is underlined in the original.

pages.full_text:
& skeletons.
	Back at the camp we had spaghetti for supper, because we'd been unable to buy bread. It was a beautiful evening.
	~~We~~ __Monday July 26th__
	We set off & stopped at __St. Cyprien__ to shop. (delicious [?boulang-] not selling pastries)

Sketched by Eve while Simon in supermarket.
photo_captions: Sketched by Eve while Simon in supermarket.
pages.notes: Page number 127 visible top-right. 'Monday July 26th' is underlined; the word before it appears struck through (possibly 'We'). 'St. Cyprien' is underlined. The page contains multiple hand-drawn pen sketches of buildings — a church with a tall steeple, smaller chapels/buildings with pointed roofs — captioned 'Sketched by Eve while Simon in supermarket.' These are architectural sketches, not a route map. The parenthetical note about the boulangerie is partially illegible; reads roughly 'delicious boulang[erie] not selling pastries'. July 26 falls on a Monday in 1993 within the 1989–2003 volume range, making 1993 the most likely year, though the year is not explicitly stated on this spread.
